name: Knowledge Islands AI Operating System
description: A governed body of cognitive work describing the Knowledge Islands AI Operating System: an architectural kernel with domain-specific modules that runs across federated large language models, keeps organisational knowledge in the modules rather than in any single model, and improves continually through a hierarchical learning loop.

executive.summary: The AI Operating System treats models as interchangeable engines and holds an organisation's knowledge, judgement, and workflows in a governed kernel plus domain modules. Work is expressed as Knowledge Packages, executed against whichever federated model best fits, and improved through a hierarchical learning loop so that lessons captured in one module raise the performance of the whole system.

assumptions: Model providers expose comparable chat and tool-use interfaces reachable over an API execution route.
The kernel and modules are versioned and distributed independently of any single model.
constraints: The system MUST remain model-independent: no module may hard-depend on a single provider.
Organisational knowledge is retained in modules and packages, never solely in a model's weights.

terminology.definition: The provider-neutral core that schedules work, enforces governance, and mediates between modules and federated models.
A domain-specific unit of knowledge and capability that extends the kernel for a particular class of work.
Routing a unit of work to whichever available model (GPT, Claude, Gemini, or other) best fits the task and route.

decisions.title: Models are engines, not stores of organisational knowledge
decisions.rationale: Keeping knowledge in modules and packages preserves it across model changes and lets the organisation switch or federate providers without loss.

decisions.title: Adopt a hierarchical continual-improvement loop
decisions.rationale: Lessons learned at the task level roll up to the module level and then to the kernel, so improvement compounds across the system.

openQuestions: What is the fallback policy when a preferred model route is unavailable mid-task?

tasks.title: Specify the kernel scheduling interface
tasks.description: Define how the kernel selects a model route for a given package and module.

tasks.title: Draft the hierarchical learning module
tasks.description: Capture how task-level lessons propagate to module and kernel scope.

deliverables.title: Kernel scheduling interface specification
deliverables.description: Normative description of route selection across federated models.

references.title: Knowledge Packages proposal (KIP-000001)
references.note: Foundational concept this package builds on.

provenance.method: Synthesised from architecture workshops and reviewed by the lead architect.

governance.promotionPolicy: Content may be promoted to canonical knowledge only after Architecture Review Board sign-off.

handover.instructions: Resume with the hierarchical learning module; the kernel interface is settled.
handover.context: The scheduling interface (T-1) is complete and its deliverable is delivered.
resumePoints: Resolve the open question on model-route fallback policy.

history.note: Kernel scheduling interface promoted to canonical knowledge after review.

related.note: Kernel scheduling interface promoted to a canonical package.
